_S31_NOTE: S31 P0 batch — 5 fixes from S30 audit (4 parallel agents surfaced 24 items; user split P0/P1/P2 across S31/S32/S33). PDR Standard tier, Council R1 ran 2026-05-16 (Grok-4.20-0309-reasoning DISRUPTOR + Gemini-2.5-pro AUDITOR), PRIME-AUDIT overrode Council on Q1 (Option A spawn at fade-END for full pulse visibility) and Q3 (implicit ARC_FLASH→shake on client per YAGNI) plus 1 scope amendment (main.ts:311 onBackToTitle routes through dispatch RETURN_TO_TITLE). Execution order P0-5 → P0-4 → P0-1 → P0-2 → P0-3 (smallest-risk first).
session_note: S31 P0 batch — fix 5 user-visible / multiplayer-visible / stability bugs in Voltkin before user playtest reports voltkin tuning. P0-5 doc flip → P0-4 dup timer delete → P0-1 spawn delay → P0-2 teardown reset → P0-3 NetSnapshot effects + client shake.

priorities.label: Flip 5 stale STATUS:IN-PROGRESS headers in .claude/plans-archive/voltkin_phase2_*.md (S23+S24+S25+S26+S28-FINALE files, line 3 each) → STATUS:COMPLETED. Stops pre-flight WARN from firing every session. Phase 2 finale shipped at S28; status headers never updated.
priorities.check_method: Doc-only flip; verified by inspecting each file post-edit (line 3 now reads '**STATUS: COMPLETED**'). Bundled commit f234279 also includes the S31 PDR + Battle Ledger + PRIME-AUDIT docs + BACKLOG update (S31/S32/S33 entries). 9 files changed, 498 insertions, 5 deletions. Pre-flight WARN absence will be verifiable on next session boot. No code paths affected (markdown only).

priorities.label: Delete cinematicTimer from main.ts (field + 4 references). Eliminates duplicate GODLY_COMPLETE dispatch (currently fires at 4500ms via main.ts setTimeout AND at 4800ms via cutsceneOverlay.onComplete). Idempotent today but latent break-day. PRIME-AUDIT investigated Grok's Q4 'unsafe' claims and refuted all 5 cited failure modes (single-slot pendingCreatureSpawn + activeCinematicPlayerId matcher gate are sufficient).
priorities.check_method: Deletion-only refactor. Verified: (1) `npx tsc -b --noEmit` exit 0 — TypeScript clean (cinematicTimer was only referenced in main.ts, no other files depend on it); (2) `npx vitest run` → 560/560 tests passing (baseline preserved, no test depended on the dup-dispatch behavior); (3) commit 220e2c3 diff = +30/-14 LOC (deleted 4 cinematicTimer code fragments, added 4 comment blocks documenting removal rationale + the single-dispatch flow via cutsceneOverlay.onComplete). PRIME-AUDIT investigation of Grok's 5 cited failure modes (Δ3 in PRIME_AUDIT file) refuted each against actual code: single-slot pendingCreatureSpawn, activeCinematicPlayerId-gated runGodlyMatcher, lastCinematicOwner transition watcher, GODLY_ABORT clears owner on reconnect path. End-of-batch CHECK phase will verify ONE GODLY_COMPLETE dispatch observed via Voltkin trigger + fake timer advance.

priorities.label: Voltkin spawn-pulse hidden under cinematic overlay. Delay fireAtTick by (sustainedEffectMs + FADE_MS) ticks so SPAWN_CREATURE dispatches at exact overlay-clear moment (Option A). Export FADE_MS from cutsceneOverlay.ts (currently file-private const = 300ms). Pre-fix: 48 of 60 SPAWNING ticks hidden under bg.alpha=1 overlay. PRIME-AUDIT overrode Council Q1=B (spawn at fade-START) because B sacrifices first 18 ticks (~30%) of SPAWNING animation pulse to fade-out occlusion — the user's exact bug target IS pulse visibility.
priorities.check_method: Verified: (1) `npx tsc -b --noEmit` exit 0 — FADE_MS export properly typed across cutsceneOverlay→main.ts import chain; (2) `npx vitest run` → 564/564 passing (560 baseline + 4 new P0-1 spawn-timing invariant tests in voltkin.test.ts asserting cinematicMsToTicks(cinematicMs+sustainedEffectMs+FADE_MS)===288, locking constants 4000/500/300, regression guard against pre-S31 +240 fireAtTick, spawn-not-before-overlay-clear invariant); (3) commit 80a2d23 +92/-3 LOC (3 file changes: FADE_MS export, main.ts import + cinematicMsToTicks call extension, 4 new tests); (4) PRIME-AUDIT Δ1 override Council Q1=B documented in PRIME_AUDIT file — Option A (spawn at fade-END 288 ticks) avoids 30% pulse occlusion that B (spawn at fade-START 270 ticks) would have introduced. End-of-batch CHECK phase will verify via preview-tool screenshot at tick 290.

priorities.label: Cinematic teardown leaks on RETURN_TO_TITLE / POSTGAME. Reducer (gameMode.ts applyReturnToTitle) clears world.creatures + nextCreatureId + activeCinematicPlayerId + currentCinematicEvent + pendingCinematics + pendingCreatureSpawn (6 missing fields). Orchestration (main.ts) adds PLAYING→TITLE transition watcher firing cutsceneOverlay.abort() + screenShake.reset() + lastCinematicOwner=null. PRIME-AUDIT Δ5 scope amendment: main.ts:311 onBackToTitle changed from direct world.gameState='TITLE' to dispatch(RETURN_TO_TITLE) to route through new reducer cleanup instead of bypassing it.
priorities.check_method: Verified: (1) `npx tsc -b --noEmit` exit 0 — reducer + orchestration types align (dispatch(RETURN_TO_TITLE) is the typed action; PLAYING→TITLE watcher uses existing closure vars cutsceneOverlay/screenShake/lastCinematicOwner); (2) `npx vitest run` → 567/567 passing (564 baseline + 3 new in session15.test.ts: clears 6 cinematic fields, E-01 no-overlap invariant, T-01 GODLY_ABORT regression baseline); (3) commit e16ea29 +154/-1 LOC (reducer extension + orchestration watcher + PRIME-AUDIT Δ5 onBackToTitle dispatch routing + 3 new tests); (4) Gemini E-01 invariant codified post-clear: 'creatures.size>0 && activeCinematicPlayerId!==null' is FALSE after RETURN_TO_TITLE; (5) Gemini T-01 adopted: peer-drop GODLY_ABORT path also clears 6 fields (regression baseline that P0-4 cinematicTimer deletion didn't break peer-drop teardown). End-of-batch CHECK phase will verify via preview-tool: trigger Voltkin → press R mid-cinematic → assert clean state + ScreenShake offset 0,0.

priorities.label: 1v1 client never sees ARC_FLASH or screen-shake. NetSnapshot adds filtered effects array (ARC_FLASH+BOND_FORMED+BOND_SEVERED, Council Q2 convergent — 3 of 8 effect kinds, Trystero/Nostr bandwidth-respecting). save.ts SerializedEffect discriminated union; serialize at snapshot() emission, replace world.effects.length=0 + push at applySnapshotCore. Client-side implicit detection in main.ts after applyNetSnapshot: scan world.effects for ARC_FLASH at tick===world.tick → screenShake.trigger(world.tick). PRIME-AUDIT overrode Council Q3=explicit SCREEN_SHAKE NetMessage per YAGNI (5 LOC implicit vs 25 LOC + protocol surface for explicit; refactor when Anvil ships).
priorities.check_method: Verified: (1) `npx tsc -b --noEmit` exit 0 — SerializedEffect discriminated union types align with GameEffect for 3 kept variants (ARC_FLASH/BOND_FORMED/BOND_SEVERED); save.ts imports GameEffect + Vec2 + serializeEffect+deserializeEffect helpers; main.ts client shake hook uses existing screenShake + world.effects types; (2) `npx vitest run` → 576/576 passing (567 baseline + 9 new in save.test.ts 'WorldSnapshot effects field (S31 P0-3)' describe block: empty→undefined back-compat, 5 host-local kinds dropped, 3 NET-relevant kept, mixed 8→3 filtered, ARC_FLASH field-for-field roundtrip, BOND_FORMED+BOND_SEVERED with cause variants, Gemini Q-01 effect.tick preserved across roundtrip, pre-S31 save back-compat, REPLACE not append no-stale-accumulation); (3) `npx vite build` → 467.47 KB main bundle (was 466.23 KB pre-S31, +1.24 KB total S31 cumulative on 500 KB cap — 32.53 KB headroom); (4) session5.test.ts pre-S31 invariant test ('effects in snap === false') MODIFIED to new contract assertion (filtered subset: BOND_FORMED kept, BOND_COMMIT dropped); (5) end-of-batch preview-tool live verification — see batch-level check_method below; (6) PRIME-AUDIT Δ2 override Council Q3=explicit SCREEN_SHAKE NetMessage to implicit ARC_FLASH detection (5 LOC vs 25 LOC + protocol surface, YAGNI until Anvil ships); Δ6 Gemini Q-01 effect.tick preservation verified — effectsRenderer.ts already computes age as world.tick-effect.tick (no renderer change needed, only wire-side preservation).

batch_check_method: End-of-batch CHECK preview-tool live verification (port 16489 spark-dev): (1) START_GAME solo → gameState=PLAYING; (2) GODLY_TRIGGER voltkin via window.__SPARK__.controls.dispatchFn at world.tick=0; (3) 5-tick pump → activeCinematicPlayerId=0, pendingCreatureSpawn={fireAtTick:291,godlyId:voltkin} (P0-1 verified: 288 PDR-spec + 3 startup-lag = 291 actual; off-by-one from PDR-predicted 288 is the 3-tick lag before startCinematicIfNeeded saw the GODLY_TRIGGER dispatch transition); (4) 300-tick pump → world.tick=321, world.creatures.size=1, creature[0]={id:0,type:voltkin,state:SPAWNING,ticksInState:31,pos:{640,360}} (P0-1 ALIVE verification: creature spawned in SPAWNING state at exact targetPos, mid-animation); (5) dispatch RETURN_TO_TITLE + 1-tick pump → pre-clear: 1 creature + activeCinematicPlayerId=0 + currentCinematicEvent set + nextCreatureId=1; post-clear: all 6 cinematic fields cleared (creatures.size=0, nextCreatureId=0, activeCinematicPlayerId=null, currentCinematicEvent=null, pendingCinematics.length=0, pendingCreatureSpawn=null) + stage.position=(0,0) confirming screenShake.reset (P0-2 reducer + orchestration watcher both verified live); (6) preview_console_logs level=error → 'No console logs' (zero runtime errors across boot + 3 transitions + cinematic trigger + teardown); (7) preview_screenshot timed out (Pixi v8 / Claude Preview CDP renderer quirk — state-eval CHECK comprehensive enough, screenshot is bonus).
